text: Pause to notice the small, living things you usually ignore.
Compliment a stranger without expecting anything in return.
Sit in silence for five minutes and just listen.
Visit the same tree for a few days and observe its changes.
Cook a meal with someone instead of ordering in.
Find a patch of grass and watch the world go by for five full breaths.
Cook with someone today and listen to what they choose to talk about.
Before you check your phone today, ask your hands what they were doing one minute ago.
Take a slow walk and let your eyes rest on the horizon.
Notice one small thing in your home that you haven't really looked at before.
Listen to the sound of rain on your window and imagine it's applauding you.
Start a conversation with a shopkeeper about the silliest item they've sold.
Take a different route home and greet three new faces along the way.
Find a flower that looks like it's mid-dance and mimic its movements.
Ask a child what game they're inventing today and ask to join.
Leave a kind note on a park bench for a stranger to find.
Try to mimic the sound of your favorite bird and see if anyone notices.
Take a photo of something that made you laugh today and share it with a friend.
Dance like nobody's watching in the middle of a public space.
Ask a child what their favorite thing about today was.
Go barefoot on grass and pretend you’re a secret explorer.
Tell a passing dog what your favorite snack is.
Hum a song you haven’t heard in years and see if anyone joins in.
Snap a photo of the first thing that makes you laugh today.
Leave a kind note for someone you’ll never see again.
Find a stick that looks like a wand and use it to draw smiley faces in the sand.
Hum a favorite tune while watching the sunrise paint the sky in soft pinks and golds.
Splash in a puddle after a rainstorm and giggle at the ripples you create.
Ask a friend to point out the most interesting leaf they can find and compare your favorites.
Spot a leaf that looks like a tiny hat and imagine who left it there.
Hum a tune to a passing bird and see if it sings back.
Share a silly joke with a friend you haven’t talked to in a while.
Lie on the grass and count how many different sounds you can hear.
Draw a smiley face on a pebble and leave it for someone to find.
